CMA已经对计划中的股权进行了大约15个月的调查，并于去年12月完成了第一阶段的审查。当时，该公司认为自己有足够的担忧，可以进入第二阶段的调查--仔细研究入股是否会阻碍亚马逊重新进入在线餐厅食品市场，并用它的话说，“进一步发展其在英国在线便利食品配送市场的存在”。
在监管机构开始这项工作后不久，新冠肺炎发起了影响欧洲的调查，因为这对Deliveroo的业务产生了显着影响。起初，冠状病毒的影响看起来是负面的，Deliveroo声称，如果没有亚马逊的股份，它就会倒闭。CMA同意这一分析，将其视为一家“濒临倒闭的公司”，并推断Deliveroo退出市场会对竞争造成更糟糕的影响-从而在4月份暂时清空亚马逊的股份。
今年6月，监管机构再次暂时批准了这笔交易-尽管它现在不再认为Deliveroo失败了，因为从2020年4月开始，它发现餐厅外卖市场的复苏速度快于预期，以及餐厅“组合”的转变(“转向规模较小的独立餐厅，而不是大型快餐连锁店”)-这两者都导致资金涌入Deliveroo的金库。然而，随着这家初创公司的财务经历“快速而重大的好转”，监管机构认为有必要完成一项“实质性评估”，以评估竞争风险。
现在终于得出结论，亚马逊持有的16%股权没有越过竞争风险门槛。所以贝佐斯可以破解泡沫-当然，前提是他知道什么是该死的Deliveroo。
CMA表示，其以竞争为由批准这笔交易的决定是“对亚马逊(Amazon)和Deliveroo的内部文件进行广泛分析，对3000多名消费者进行调查，以及感兴趣的第三方提交大量意见书的结果”。
该公司表示，评估着眼于亚马逊持有16%的股份将如何“影响其在未来几年在餐厅送货和在线便利杂货送货方面与Deliveroo独立竞争的动机”。
“CMA最终发现，这一投资水平不会大幅减少任何一个市场的竞争。然而，如果亚马逊获得对Deliveroo的更大程度的控制--例如，通过收购该公司的控股权--这可能引发CMA的进一步调查，“该公司补充道。
调查主席斯图尔特·麦金托什(Stuart McIntosh)在一份声明中进一步发表评论称：“考虑到第二阶段适用的更高法律标准，集团得出的结论是，这笔交易不会导致餐厅送货或方便杂货送货方面的竞争大幅减弱。”
麦金托什还煞费苦心地强调，这一决定反映了投资的规模，以及亚马逊“在两个市场竞争的动机”--他重申警告称，如果亚马逊试图增加其在Deliveroo的份额，可能会引发新的调查。
亚马逊宣布将牵头对英国外卖应用业务进行5.75亿美元的G系列投资，这一消息可以追溯到2019年5月。
此举标志着这家电商巨头在英国外卖市场的第二次行动。早在2016年，这家电商巨头就与伦敦餐厅一起为Prime会员推出了按需送餐服务。然而，在面临Deliveroo和Uber Eats等公司的激烈竞争后，它在几年后停止了这一努力。
在回应CMA对亚马逊股份的清理时，Deliveroo强调，“CMA调查所依据的五种‘伤害理论’都没有得到证实”。
我们很高兴CMA已经结束了15个月的调查，亚马逊的少数股权投资现在可以继续进行。
这对英国顾客和餐馆以及英国经济来说都是极好的消息。英国出生的Deliveroo将利用这笔投资为客户增加选择和价值，为餐厅提供支持，并将能够随着公司的扩张为更多的乘客提供他们重视的灵活工作。
让Deliveroo感到兴奋的是，亚马逊作为世界上最专注于客户、最具创新力的公司，对Deliveroo表现出如此巨大的信任票，并选择投资于公司的未来。
该公司提供了一些最新的商业指标，称目前全球有10万家餐厅在其平台上，仅今年就有3万家餐厅加入-该公司声称，这表明“Covid危机在多大程度上让餐厅将送货作为一项重要的收入来源”。
它补充说：“在全球与Deliveroo合作的7.5万家餐厅中，有7.5万家是受疫情影响最严重的小型独立餐厅。”
note_en:
The  CMA   had been investigating the planned stake for some 15 months, completing phase one of its scrutiny in December. At the time it decided it had enough concerns to move to a phase 2 probe — chewing over whether or not the stake might discourage Amazon from re-entering the online restaurant food market and “further developing its presence within the online convenience grocery delivery market in the UK”, as it put it.
 Soon after the regulator started in on this work COVID-19 struck Europe — impacting investigation as it had a marked impact on  Deliveroo’s   business. Initially the impact of the coronavirus looked negative, with Deliveroo claiming it would have gone out of business without Amazon’s stake. The CMA concurred with this analysis, treating it as a “failing firm” and reasoning that Deliveroo’s exit from the market would have been worse for competition — thereby provisionally clearing the Amazon stake in  April.
  Then again in  June the regulator provisionally cleared the deal — although it now no longer considered Deliveroo failing, being as, from April 2020, it found a sharper than expected recovery in the restaurant food delivery market, as well as a shift in the restaurant ‘mix’ (“towards smaller, independent restaurants and away from large fast food chains”) — both of which resulted in money being poured into Deliveroo’s coffers. Yet then — with the startup’s finances experiencing “rapid and significant turnaround” — the regulator felt it necessary to complete a “substantive assessment” to of the risks to competition.
 Now it’s finally concluded that Amazon’s 16% stake does not cross the competitive risk threshold. So Bezos can crack out the bubble — assuming he knows what the heck Deliveroo is of course.
 The CMA  said its decision to clear the deal on competition grounds is “the culmination of extensive analysis of internal documents from Amazon and Deliveroo, a survey of more than 3,000 consumers, and extensive submissions from interested third parties”.
 It said the assessment looked at how a 16% shareholding by Amazon would “affect its incentives to compete independently with Deliveroo in both restaurant delivery and online convenience grocery delivery in the coming years”.
 “The CMA ultimately found that this level of investment will not substantially lessen competition in either market. However, if Amazon were to acquire a greater level of control over Deliveroo — through, for example, acquiring a controlling interest in the company — this could trigger a further investigation by the CMA,” it added.
 Commenting further in a statement, Stuart McIntosh, inquiry chair, said: “ Taking account of the higher legal standard that applies at Phase 2, the Group has concluded that the transaction will not result in a substantial lessening of competition in either restaurant delivery or convenience grocery delivery.”
 McIntosh was also at pains to emphasize that the decision  reflects the scale of the investment and Amazon ‘s “incentives to compete in both markets” — reiterating the warning that should Amazon try to increase its share of Deliveroo a fresh investigation may be triggered.
 The announcement that Amazon was leading a $575 million Series G investment in the UK food delivery app business dates back to  May 2019.
 The move signalled a second act for the ecommerce behemoth in the UK food delivery market, after it launched an on-demand food delivery offer with London restaurants for Prime members back in  2016. However it went on to shutter the effort a  couple of years later — having faced fierce competition from the likes of Deliveroo and Uber Eats.
 Responding to the CMA’s clearance of the Amazon stake, Deliveroo emphasized that “none of the five ‘Theories of Harm’ on which the CMA based its investigation have been substantiated”.
  We are delighted that the CMA has concluded its 15 month investigation and that the Amazon minority investment can now go ahead.
 This is fantastic news for UK customers and restaurants, and for the British economy. British born Deliveroo will use the investment to increase choice and value for customers, support for restaurants and will be able to offer more riders the flexible work they value as the company expands.
 Deliveroo is excited that Amazon, the most customer-obsessed and innovative company in the world, has shown such a huge vote of confidence in Deliveroo and chosen to invest in the company’s future.
 The company offered some updated business metrics, saying there are now 100,000 restaurants on its platform globally, with 30,000 joining this year alone — which it claimed points to “the extent to which the Covid crisis has seen restaurants turn to delivery as a vital source of revenue”.
 “75,000 of the restaurants who work with Deliveroo globally are small, independent restaurants who have been hit hardest by the pandemic,” it added.
title: 亚马逊咬下Deliveroo 16%的股份最终澄清了英国的竞争调查
title_en: Amazon’s 16% bite of Deliveroo finally clears UK competition probe

note:
这轮融资之所以吸引了我们的眼球，是因为AgentSync所在的领域在2020年出现了一波引人注目的风险投资热潮-保险技术，我们对此进行了相当广泛的报道-还因为它分享了我们喜欢的硬收入数字。
那么，让我们来谈谈该公司的联合创始人詹恩·奈特和尼吉·萨巴瓦尔是如何为保险市场开发软件的。
AgentSync提供它所说的“合规即服务”，帮助保险运营商和保险机构跟踪保险经纪人许可数据。对于习惯于用电子表格做这项工作的公司来说，AgentSync提供了一种建立在Salesforce平台之上的更快的方法，节省了时间，降低了出错的机会。
萨巴瓦尔是这家臭名昭著的初创公司的早期员工。听这位AgentSync联合创始人讲述这个故事，Zenefits以非人的速度增长，从萨巴瓦尔加入时的100名员工增加到一年后的1700多名员工。
在其高速增长期间，后来公布的报告显示，Zenefits没有充分认识到它是在一个高度监管的行业中运营的。由此造成的合规混乱迫使联合创始人帕克·康拉德离开公司，Yammer前老板大卫·萨克斯接手清理内部事务。
据TechCrunch报道，在萨克斯接管Zenefits时，他曾写信给Zenefits员工，称“合规就像氧气”，没有合规，公司将“死亡”。
康拉德被SEC罚款，Zenefits裁员，不得不重新估值。萨克斯最终离开了公司。但在头条背后，萨巴瓦尔描述了从内到外以更合规的方式重建Zenefits的工作。他在一次采访中说，这些努力的一部分是开发帮助跟踪代理合规的软件，Zenefits后来开源并发布了这个项目。
TechCrunch当时报道了这一版本，并写道，Zenefits已经建立了“一个内部创建的许可合规应用程序，以确保其销售人员获得适当的许可，可以在给定的州销售保险，任何人都可以从Salesforce App Exchange免费下载。”
该软件与国家保险生产者登记(NIPR)数据集成，联合创始人将其描述为保险市场的真相来源。该软件允许用户确认各个代理是否合规。这一努力为Zenefits赢得了监管机构的一些赞誉，根据Sabharwal的说法，还有其他希望使用该软件的公司。
从内部软件项目和外部需求的契合点出发，AgentSync成立，Sabharwal离开Zenefits，与他的合作伙伴Knight一起创办了自己的公司。奈特曾在Dropbox(业务技术主管)和Strip(内部系统主管)工作过，在今年全职加入AgentSync之前，他曾在AgentSync做过兼职工作。
Zenefits在他的团队为AgenySync编写任何代码之前，将早期项目的IP签署给了Sabharwal，使公司能够有一个全新的开始。
保险市场是巨大的，有利可图的，而且是老式的。这使它成为攻击的最佳空间。该软件还帮助机载代理小组执行创业公司所称的“自动生成的合规性分析”，以帮助发现差距和其他数据错误。
AgentSync正在发挥吸引力，在发表时已增至190万美元的年度经常性收入(ARR)。该公司对客户拥有的每个活跃代理收取费用，并根据规模进行一些价格分级。
如今，这家初创公司已有17人，目标是在今年年底前达到22人。(它正在投资于融资后的上市功能。)。
在人事方面，该公司的首席技术官奈特(Knight)已经建立了一支以女性为主的技术团队，不幸的是，这在行业中是罕见的。她还说，她“敏锐地意识到整个行业中女性和代表性不足群体存在的公平和薪酬差距”。
我没有机会与太多来自Zenefits校友的居民交谈，但AgentSync有趣的是，它的诞生实际上是为了解决独角兽公司的问题。而且，它已经为这种修复找到了市场。让我们看看它能用440万美元走多远。
note_en:
The round caught our eye because  AgentSync   is working in a space that has seen a notable wave of venture interest in 2020 — insurtech, which we’ve  covered somewhat  extensively — and because it shared hard revenue numbers, which we love.
 So let’s talk about how the company’s co- founders  Jenn Knight and  Niji Sabharwal wound up building  software for the insurance market.
   AgentSync offers what it describes as “compliance as a service,” helping insurance carriers and insurance agencies track insurance broker licensing data. For companies accustomed to doing this work with spreadsheets, AgentSync offers a faster method, built on top of Salesforce’s platform, saving time and lowering the chance of error.
   Sabharwal was an early-employee at the infamous startup. To hear the AgentSync co-founder tell the story, Zenefits grew at an inhuman clip, scaling from 100 employees when Sabharwal joined to over 1,700 a year later.
 During its period of hyper-growth, reporting later uncovered, Zenefits did not sufficiently appreciate that it operated in a highly-regulated industry. The resulting compliance mess forced co-founder Parker Conrad from the company, with former Yammer boss David Sacks taking the reins to clean house.
 At the time of his takeover, TechCrunch reported that  Sacks wrote to Zenefits staff that “compliance is like oxygen,” and that without the company would “die.”
 Conrad  got fined by the SEC, Zenefits  cut staff, and  had to re-value itself.  Sacks eventually left the company. But behind the headlines Sabharwal described work to rebuild Zenefits in a more compliant fashion from the inside-out. Part of those efforts, he said in an interview, was building software that helped track agent compliance, a project that Zenefits later open-sourced and released.
 TechCrunch  covered the release at the time, writing that Zenefits had built “a licensing compliance app it created in-house to ensure its sales people are properly licensed to sell insurance in a given state available for free to anyone to download from the Salesforce App Exchange.”
 The software integrated with National Insurance Producer Registry ( NIPR) data, which the co-founder describes as a source of truth in the insurance market. The software allowed users to confirm that individual agents were compliant. The effort bought Zenefits some kudos with regulators, and, according to Sabharwal, other companies looking to use the software.
 From the meeting point of internal software project and external demand, AgentSync was formed, with Sabharwal leaving Zenefits to start his company with his partner, Knight. Knight, who has done stints at Dropbox (Head of Business Technology) and Stripe (Head of Internal Systems), worked part-time at AgentSync before joining the startup full-time this year.
 Zenefits signed the IP from the earlier project over to Sabharwal before his team wrote any code for AgenySync, allowing the company to get a clean start.
 The insurance market is enormous, lucrative, and old-fashioned. That makes it a prime space to attack. The software also helps groups onboard agents, execute what the startup calls “automatically-generated compliance analysis” to help spot gaps and other data errors.
 And AgentSync is seeing traction, scaling to $1.9 million annual recurring revenue (ARR) at the time of publication. The company charges per active agent a customer has, with some price tiering based on scale.
 Today the startup has 17 people, and is targeting 22 by the end of the year. (It’s investing in its go-to-market functionality post-fundraising.)
 On the personnel side, Knight, the company’s CTO, has built a technical team that is majority women, an unfortunately a rarity in the industry. She also said that she’s “acutely aware of the equity and pay gaps that exist for women and underrepresented groups across the industry.”
 I haven’t had the chance to talk to too many denizens from the Zenefits alumni, but what’s fun about AgentSync is that it was born effectively out of an effort to fix what went wrong at the unicorn. And, it’s found a market for that fix. Let’s see how far it can get on $4.4 million.

note:
该公司最近的首席执行官凯伦·皮科克告诉TechCrunch，她的新任首席财务官丹·格里格斯是一个强有力的候选人，这要归功于他帮助火箭燃料公司上市的经验，以及他在Sitecore公司帮助执行“整个业务转型”的经验，他在进入内部通信公司之前曾在Sitecore工作过。
Intercom是一家软件初创公司，销售与支持和营销团队合作的客户聊天软件。其服务的不同层级允许自动的“对话”活动，以及定制的机器人。根据Crunchbase的数据，该公司已经筹集了超过2.9亿美元的资金。
格里格斯告诉TechCrunch，他不是来找新角色的，但与孔雀的对话吸引了他。
皮科克在担任公司首席运营官约三年后接任首席执行官一职，在此期间，人们知道前任首席执行官对员工做出了“不必要的举动”。在孔雀掌舵之前，对讲机公司也进行了裁员。据报道，该公司在5月份裁减了大约6%的员工，当时许多科技公司都在裁员，原因是围绕新冠肺炎的市场不确定性和它的经济混乱。
根据TechCrunch在皮科克接任首席执行官时的报告，对讲机每年的经常性收入(ARR)约为1.5亿美元。该公司向TechCrunch澄清，ARR里程碑是在上一财年结束时，即2020年1月结束时达到的。
格里格斯说，对讲机已经接近盈利，并且正以“强劲”的两位数增长。我们将其解读为50%至99%的增长，这意味着该公司可能会在本财年(2021年1月)结束时实现2.25亿至2.985亿美元的ARR，但由于大数定律，它可能会偏向较小的数字。
有了一位有IPO经验的CFO，有了新的CEO，有了坚实的收入基础和良好的增长，IPO是什么时候？遗憾的是，不会很快。这位首席财务官说，他的公司不需要筹集新资本，今天有足够的流动性进行投资。这是金融用语，意思是“不着急”。
这位首席执行官也持相同立场，他在同一次电话会议上表示，内部通信并不急于上市，希望在执行交易之前建立一些内部基础设施。她估计，至少在12个月内不会有首次公开募股(IPO)。1个。
对讲机在2020年遭遇了一些市场冲击，在过去一年左右的时间里，不得不花了一些时间来清理内部问题。现在，从理论上讲，它已经对房子进行了分类，并在一个最近几个季度为软件初创公司带来巨大回报的市场上运营，特别是那些帮助其他公司进行数字化运营的公司。
让我们看看对讲机能以多快的速度增长。我们会得到完整的回顾展和最终的S-1。
note_en:
The company’s recent CEO,  Karen Peacock, told TechCrunch that her new CFO  Dan Griggs was a strong candidate thanks to his experience helping  take Rocket Fuel public, and for helping execute a “whole business transformation” at  Sitecore, where he worked immediately before coming to Intercom.
 Intercom   is a software startup that sells customer-chat software that works with support and marketing teams. Different tiers of its service allow for automated “ conversational” campaigns, and custom bots. The company has raised  a hair over $290 million, according to Crunchbase data.
  Griggs told TechCrunch that he was not in the market for a new role, but conversations with Peacock drew him in.
 Peacock  took over the CEO role after around three years as the company’s COO, during which time it became known that the preceding CEO had made “ unwanted advances” on employees. Intercom company also  underwent layoffs before Peacock took over the helm. According to reporting, the firm cut around 6% of its staff in May, a time when many tech companies were trimming personnel due to market uncertainties surrounding COVID-19 and its economic disruptions.
  According to  TechCrunch reporting at the time when Peacock took over as CEO, Intercom had around $150 million in annual recurring revenue (ARR). The company clarified to TechCrunch that the ARR milestone was reached at the end of its last fiscal year, or the conclusion of January of 2020.
  Intercom, Griggs said, is near profitability and is growing in the “strong” double digits. We read that as meaning between 50% and 99% growth, implying the company could close its current fiscal year (January 2021) with $225 million to $298.5 million in ARR, with a bias — thanks to the laws of large numbers — towards the smaller figure.
 With a CFO with IPO experience on hand, a new CEO, a material revenue base and good growth, when is the IPO? Not soon, sadly. The CFO said his company doesn’t need to raise new capital, and that it has enough liquidity today to invest. That’s financial-speak for “no rush.”
 The CEO is on the same page, saying during the same call that Intercom is not in a hurry to go public, and wants to build out some internal infrastructure before executing the transaction. There won’t be an IPO for at least twelve months, she estimated. 1
 Intercom hit some market chop in 2020 and had to spend parts of the last year or so cleaning up internal issues. Now, in theory, it has sorted house, and is operating in a market that  has greatly rewarded software startups in recent quarters, especially those helping other companies operate digitally.
 Let’s see how fast Intercom can grow. We’ll get the full retrospective with its eventual S-1.
title: Intercom在准备IPO时聘请了一名CFO

今天，该公司宣布了一项480万美元的种子投资，由CRV牵头，参与投资的有Paladin Capital Group和几个个人科技高管投资者。
“通常情况下，你看到的警报中约有20%(不需要你的注意)。这些警报是由好人和坏人产生的，他们投机取巧地扫描、爬行、探查和攻击互联网上的每一台设备。“GreyNoise创始人安德鲁·莫里斯告诉TechCrunch。
他补充说，“这就产生了背景噪音问题，所以我们基本上收集了所有那些在整个互联网上扫描和爬行每个人的人的所有数据，进行分析，然后从我们的客户看到的内容中过滤出来。”因此，他们最终得到的是大约20%的警报，更少的警报。“。
令人惊讶的是，该公司没有使用机器学习来做到这一点(尽管添加机器学习元素是路线图上的内容)。相反，莫里斯说，它涉及到对传感器数据的大量自动分析。
“我们有一个巨大的收集器传感器网络，位于互联网上所有这些不同的数据中心和数百个数据中心。我们只是对流量应用了一系列规则，他们都认为这些规则最终会导致我们核心产品的产出。“他说。
他说，随着公司推进这一新的融资，他主要希望摆脱这种方式，从客户那里获得更多数据，以换取订阅成本的折扣。
“展望未来，对我们来说，收集我们想要的所有第一手数据的成本令人望而却步。因此，我们将不得不开始基本上建造产品，使我们的用户能够为我们收集数据。这就是我们将利用这笔资金建立起来的东西。“莫里斯说。
此外，他们还将与ISP和数据中心所有者等其他主要供应商合作，帮助他们收集更多数据。
有趣的是，这完全是与CRV的里德·克里斯蒂安(Reid Christian)进行的COVID交易，他从未亲自与莫里斯会面，而是通过Zoom进行整个过程。“这是时代的征兆，安德鲁和我从来没有见过面，很可能在很长一段时间内也不会见面。克里斯蒂安在宣布这笔交易的博客文章中写道：“我们在隔离期间建立了联系，我们两人都躲在我们的公寓(分别是华盛顿和SF)里，在那里我们坐在无数个Zoom电话上，大多是在GreyNoise之前相互了解并讨论机会，”克里斯蒂安在宣布这笔交易的博客文章中写道。
到目前为止，这家初创公司有7名员工。莫里斯说，他计划在明年招聘10人，重点是销售、营销和工程。他说，随着他招聘更多的人，在公司的早期阶段考虑多样性和包容性是当务之急。
“要做到这一点，最好的方法是从一开始就尽可能地多样化地招聘员工，因为事后要让一家公司变得更加多样化，要比从一开始就考虑包容性和多样性要难得多。”这就是我们现在所做的每一次招聘都是如何考虑一切的。“他说。在他扩大公司的过程中，这将如何运作仍是他正在考虑的问题，他计划与研发专家合作，帮助充实一项计划。
莫里斯于2017年在华盛顿特区地区创立了该公司，并于2018年推出了该产品的第一个版本，目前已拥有40名客户。
note_en:
Today, the company announced a $4.8 million seed investment led by  CRV   with participation from Paladin Capital Group and several individual tech executive investors.
 “Usually about 20% of the alerts that you’re looking at [don’t require your attention]. And those alerts are generated by both good guys and bad guys who are opportunistically scanning and crawling and probing and attacking every single device all around the internet,” GreyNoise founder Andrew Morris told TechCrunch.
  He adds, “It creates this background noise problem, so we basically collect all of that data from all of those people who are scanning and crawling everybody on the entire internet, analyze it and we filter it out from what our customers see. So what they end up with is about 20%, fewer alerts.”
 Surprisingly, the company is not using machine learning to do this (although adding machine learning elements is on the roadmap). Instead, Morris says it involves a lot of automated analysis of sensor data.
 “We have a giant network of collector sensors that are sitting in all these different data centers all around the internet and hundreds of data centers around the internet. And we’re just applying a bunch of rules to the traffic that they all see to end up with the output of our core product,” he said.
 As the company moves forward with this new funding, he says primarily he wants to get away from this approach and get more data from customers in exchange for discounts on their subscription costs.
 “Moving forward, it’s cost prohibitive for us to collect all of the data that we want firsthand. So we’re going to have to start basically building products that are enabling our users to collect data for us. And that’s something that we’re going to be building out using this funding,” Morris said.
 In addition, they will be partnering with other key vendors like ISPs and data center owners to help them collect additional data.
 Interestingly, this was an entirely COVID transaction with CRV’s Reid Christian never meeting Morris in person, conducting the entire process over Zoom. “A sign of the times, Andrew and I have never met in person and likely won’t for quite some time. We were connected in the midst of quarantine, both of us holed up in our apartments (DC and SF, respectively) where we sat on countless Zoom calls, mostly getting to know each other and discussing the opportunity ahead of GreyNoise,” Christian wrote in a blog post announcing the deal.
 The startup has 7 employees to this point. Morris said that he has plans to hire 10 people in the next year with an emphasis on sales, marketing and engineering. As he hires more people, he says it’s imperative to be thinking about diversity and inclusion in his hiring in the early stages of the company.
 “The best way to do this is to hire as diverse as humanly possible from the very beginning, because it’s significantly harder to make a company more diverse after the fact than it is to think about inclusion and diversity from the very beginning. And so that’s how we’ve been thinking about everything right now with every hire that we’re doing,” he said. How that will work as he builds out the company is still something he is considering and he plans work with D&I experts to help flesh out a plan.
 Morris founded the company in the Washington, D.C. area in 2017, came to market in 2018 with the first version of the product and today has 40 customers.

从7月底开始，微软已经开始检测阻止Windows10遥测服务器的主机文件，认为这是一个严重的安全风险。
HOSTS文件是一个文本文件，位于C：\WINDOWS\SYSTEM32\DRIVER\ETC\HOSTS，并且只能由具有管理员权限的程序编辑。
此文件用于在不使用域名系统(DNS)的情况下将主机名解析为IP地址。
此文件通常用于通过将主机分配给127.0.0.1或0.0.0.0 IP地址来阻止计算机访问远程站点。
例如，如果您将以下行添加到Windows主机文件，它将阻止用户访问www.google.com，因为您的浏览器会认为您正在尝试连接到本地计算机127.0.0.1。
自7月底以来，Windows10用户开始报告说，Windows defender已经开始检测修改后的主机文件是否对&#39；SettingsModifier:Win32/HostsFileHijack&#39；构成威胁。
检测到后，如果用户单击了查看详细信息选项，则只会显示他们受到设置修改器威胁的影响，并且具有潜在的有害行为，如下所示。
Beepingcomputer最初是从BornCity了解到这个问题的，虽然Microsoft Defender检测主机劫持并不是什么新鲜事，但看到这么多人突然报告检测到这个问题是很奇怪的[1，2，3，4，5]。
虽然过去同时袭击许多消费者的大范围感染并不是闻所未闻的，但对于今天Windows 10内置的安全性来说，这是相当不寻常的。
这让我相信这是假阳性或其他非恶意问题。
在玩过诸如屏蔽beepingcomputer和其他站点之类的通用主机文件修改后，我尝试在主机文件中添加一个针对微软遥测的阻止列表。
此列表添加了Windows操作系统和Microsoft软件用来将遥测和用户数据发回Microsoft的许多Microsoft服务器。
保存主机文件后，我立即收到以下警报，指出无法保存该文件，因为它包含病毒或可能不需要的软件。&#34；我还收到了我的计算机感染了&#39；SettingsModifier:Win32/HostsFileHijack.&#39；&#39；的警报。
因此，微软最近似乎没有更新他们的Microsoft Defender定义，以检测他们的服务器何时添加到主机文件中。
利用HOSTS文件拦截Windows10遥测的用户突然看到HOSTS文件劫持检测。
在我们的测试中，在Windows 10主机文件中检测到的一些Microsoft主机包括：
Www.microsoft.com microsoft.com telemetry.microsoft.com wns.notfy.windows.com.akadns.net v10-win.vortex.data.microsoft.com.akadns.net us.vortex-win.data.microsoft.com us-v10.events.data.microsoft.com urs.microsoft.com.nsatc.net watson.telemetry.microsoft.com watson.ppe.telemetry.microsoft.com vsGall.。
如果您决定清除此威胁，Microsoft将把HOSTS文件还原为其默认内容。
有意修改其主机文件的用户可以允许此威胁，但它可能会导致所有主机修改，甚至恶意修改。
因此，只有在您100%了解这样做所涉及的风险的情况下才允许威胁。
note_en:
Starting at the end of July, Microsoft has begun detecting HOSTS files that block Windows 10 telemetry servers as a 'Severe' security risk.
 The HOSTS file is a text file located at C:\Windows\system32\driver\etc\HOSTS and can only be edited by a program with Administrator privileges.
 This file is used to resolve hostnames to IP addresses without using the Domain Name System (DNS).
 This file is commonly used to block a computer from accessing a remote site by assigning host to the 127.0.0.1 or 0.0.0.0 IP address.
 For example, if you add the following line to the Windows HOSTS file, it will block users from accessing www.google.com as your browsers will think you are trying to connect to 127.0.0.1, which is the local computer.
   Since the end of July, Windows 10 users began reporting that Windows Defender had started detecting modified HOSTS files as a 'SettingsModifier:Win32/HostsFileHijack' threat.
 When detected, if a user clicks on the 'See details' option, they will simply be shown that they are affected by a 'Settings Modifier' threat and has 'potentially unwanted behavior,' as shown below.
  BleepingComputer first learned about this issue  from BornCity, and while Microsoft Defender detecting HOSTS hijacks  is not new, it was strange to see so many people suddenly reporting the detection [ 1,  2,  3,  4,  5].
 While a widespread infection hitting many consumers simultaneously in the past is not unheard of, it is quite unusual with the security built into Windows 10 today.
 This led me to believe it was a false positive or some other non-malicious issue.
 After playing with generic HOSTS file modifications such as blocking BleepingComputer and other sites, I tried adding a  blocklist for Microsoft's telemetry to my HOSTS file.
 This list adds many Microsoft servers used by the Windows operating system and Microsoft software to send telemetry and user data back to Microsoft.
 As soon as I saved the HOSTS file, I received the following alert stating that I could not save the file as it "contains a virus or potentially unwanted software." I also received alerts that my computer was infected with 'SettingsModifier:Win32/HostsFileHijack.''
  So it seems that Microsoft had recently updated their Microsoft Defender definitions to detect when their servers were added to the HOSTS file.
 Users who utilize HOSTS files to block Windows 10 telemetry suddenly caused them to see the HOSTS file hijack detection.
 In our tests, some of the Microsoft hosts detected in the Windows 10 HOSTS file include the following:
 www.microsoft.commicrosoft.comtelemetry.microsoft.comwns.notify.windows.com.akadns.netv10-win.vortex.data.microsoft.com.akadns.netus.vortex-win.data.microsoft.comus-v10.events.data.microsoft.comurs.microsoft.com.nsatc.netwatson.telemetry.microsoft.comwatson.ppe.telemetry.microsoft.comvsgallery.comwatson.live.comwatson.microsoft.comtelemetry.remoteapp.windowsazure.comtelemetry.urs.microsoft.com
 If you decide to clean this threat, Microsoft will restore the HOSTS file back to its default contents.
  Users who intentionally modify their HOSTS file can allow this 'threat,' but it may enable all HOSTS modifications, even malicious ones, going forward.
 So only allow the threat if you 100% understand the risks involved in doing so.
title: Windows 10：主机文件阻止遥测现在被标记为存在风险
title_en: Windows 10: HOSTS File Blocking Telemetry Is Now Flagged As a Risk

微软将于9月15日在Android设备上独家推出名为Project XCloud的游戏流媒体服务。Project XCloud将作为Xbox Game Pass旗舰版订阅的一部分捆绑在一起，价格为每月14.99美元，并承诺在Android平板电脑和手机上提供100多款游戏。游戏流媒体最初将仅限于Android；iPhone和iPad用户将不得不等待。
微软一位发言人在给The Verge的一份声明中表示：“我们的雄心是通过Xbox Game Pass在所有设备上扩展云游戏，但目前我们没有更多关于iOS的信息可供分享。”IPhone和iPad用户在发布时缺乏对iOS的支持，这会让他们感到失望，但这并不完全令人惊讶。虽然微软一直在iOS上测试XCloud，但该公司此前透露，由于苹果应用商店的政策，其测试是有限的。

note_en:
Microsoft will launch its game streaming service, known as Project xCloud, on September 15th exclusively on Android devices. Project xCloud will be  bundled as part of the Xbox Game Pass Ultimate subscription, priced at $14.99 per month, with a promise of more than 100 games available on Android tablets and phones. Game streaming will be limited initially to Android; iPhone and iPad users will have to wait.
 “It’s our ambition to scale cloud gaming through Xbox Game Pass available on all devices, but we have nothing further to share at this time regarding iOS,” says a Microsoft spokesperson in a statement to  The Verge. The lack of iOS support at launch will be disappointing to iPhone and iPad owners but not exactly surprising. While  Microsoft has been testing xCloud on iOS, the company previously revealed its testing was limited due to Apple’s App Store policies.
 Microsoft hasn’t revealed which Apple policies are preventing xCloud from launching on iOS, but Google’s Stadia service is also not available on iPhones or iPads.  Valve’s Steam Link app took nearly a year for Apple to approve even though it primarily streams games from your home PC. Apple has also faced questions over its  30 percent cut of in-app purchases and strict App Store policies from the House Judiciary Committee recently as part of a  tech antitrust hearing.
   Microsoft is now pushing ahead with its xCloud launch on September 15th regardless, although it appears the company is referring to it as the “cloud gaming” feature of Xbox Game Pass Ultimate. It’s launching in 22 countries next month, including Austria, Belgium, Canada, Czech Republic, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Spain, South Korea, Sweden, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, and the United States.
 The Cloud gaming feature, or xCloud as we know it today, will also launch as a beta while Microsoft works to scale it to the millions it expects to use the service around the world. Microsoft will end its free xCloud preview on September 11th, and the company is planning to launch an updated Xbox Game Pass app with game streaming on September 15th. Interestingly, the versions that will be published on the One Store in Korea and Galaxy Store for Samsung users globally will both include “in-app purchase capabilities,” according to Microsoft, suggesting that the Google Play Store version will not.
 Microsoft is also partnering with Razer, PowerA,  8BitDo, and Nacon to create accessories and controllers that are ready for xCloud. You’ll also be able to use existing Xbox One controllers or even Sony’s DualShock 4 controllers to play games on xCloud. Microsoft isn’t detailing all 100-plus games that will be available for game streaming just yet, but the company is providing a list of 36 titles that are coming:
title: 微软的XCloud游戏流媒体服务将于9月15日在22个国家的Android上推出，并与Xbox Games Pass Ultimate订阅捆绑在一起
title_en: Microsoft's xCloud game streaming service will launch on September 15 on Android in 22 countries, bundled with the Xbox Games Pass Ultimate subscription

现代顶尖公司的领导人一度都是初创企业家，他们只梦想建立一家有利可图的企业。每个领导都必须学习如何管理人员，如何经营企业，如何处理错误，他们必须向他们学习。这些人的真实经历对商业新手和有经验的企业家都很有价值，他们不仅想让自己的公司生存下去，还想让公司成为市场领导者。
我们已经创建了一份销售、财务、商业和管理方面的优秀书籍清单，每一本书都可以成为任何级别的企业家的强大动力。
以下是创业者必读的最佳商业书籍清单。从那些持续获得高额利润的人的成功中吸取的经验教训会让你思考未来，以及你如何现在就开始把它变得更好。
这本书是用简单的语言写的，但它揭示了几个有趣、复杂和令人兴奋的想法。沃伦·巴菲特(Warren Buffett)--美国最著名的投资者--的独特之处在于，他的数十亿美元不是通过公司的发展获得的，而是通过投资其他公司获得的。读这本书你能学到什么？巴菲特的投资决策，他的战术，战略，他如何选择正确的投资，他如何工作，以及一个伟大的投资者的思维方式。
“巴比伦首富”是一部经典之作，但书中勾勒出的成功秘诀适用于任何活动领域。想学习如何赚钱吗？阅读这本书会让你更接近于理解金融的核心和不变的规律，理解进行有利可图的投资的秘诀，以及积累、保存和增加你的资本。
书：首先，打破所有规则：世界上最伟大的经理人有什么不同的做法。
对于学习销售和人事管理书籍的实习经理来说，这本书应该是你办公桌上的必需品。您如何发现员工的优势？你如何展示他们的才华，并最大限度地投入到他们的工作中？知识、能力、技能、经验、成就--这些概念不能混淆，也不能替换。你不能要求员工做不到的事情，但是有办法在团队中创造一个理想的工作氛围，从而确保你的公司又快又有效地发展。
新书：重温电子迷思：为什么大多数小企业行不通？该怎么办？
对于那些打算白手起家创业的人来说，这本书是天赐之物。即使没有创始人，什么样的商业模式也能顺利运作？小企业发展的秘诀是什么？您如何组织、发展和改进您的业务？所有这些你都会以一种简单易懂的形式学习。
这本书显然从其他传统商业小说中脱颖而出。它教你控制你的生活，深入分析新出现的问题，它向读者介绍限制理论，它谈论爱和风险。作者的非标准思维使“目标”这本书成为寻找常识和理性生活方式的真正教程。
最好的商业书籍不是每天都写的。几十年后，它们都必须通过时间的考验来证明它们的相关性。注意从经商权威毋庸置疑的作者那里选书。
这本来自管理大师本人的书可以作为经验丰富的管理者和初学者的一本圣经。这本书，连同它的许多实用技巧，给出了关于世界经济和人类总体发展的全球预测。“21世纪的管理任务”不是枯燥的理论，而是积极的行动号召。是落实新任务、解决紧迫问题的准备。
这本奇妙的书值得一读，它可以帮助你理解，没有一本书能解决你的问题，不管它是不是关于商业和创业的最好的书。成功的关键是这本发行量超过1000万册的畅销书的作者在成功的公司中看到的：对责任的理解，对正确事物的理解，对建设性思维的理解，以及对出人意料的大胆决定的理解。
史蒂夫·乔布斯的传记不仅在销量上大获成功，而且是一本超级畅销书。它是建立在与苹果创始人本人以及他的亲戚、朋友和敌人、祝福者和竞争对手的大量对话的基础上的。史蒂夫·乔布斯的生平故事令人兴奋，富有启发性，令人惊讶，更重要的是，它激励并促使你思考、适应和奋斗。
这本书的原名是亨利·福特，他改变了我们今天生活的世界，书名是“我的生活和工作”。它享誉世界，有一百多个版本。许多人认为这本书是亨利·福特本人的传记，但更确切地说，这是他一生工作的传记。公司发展和业务创建的原则，起伏，风险和错误，胜利和突破-一切都在这里突显通往巨大成功和全球认可的道路是多么复杂、漫长和艰难。
与纸质媒体脱节的年轻企业家经常在文学和有声读物中寻找答案，以回答一个紧迫的问题：“你如何才能从头开始创业？”--但他们这样做是徒劳的。商业想法、小贴士、理论、方法论以及真正的成功故事往往比承诺揭示全球经济秘密的昂贵的长期课程更有效。
想出一个商业点子并付诸实施并不是最困难的事情。推广一种产品，将其推向市场，并释放出螺旋式的增长和发展，难度要大得多。在真实消费者身上快速测试新产品，即时调整业务发展策略，是本书作者向大家展示的建议之一，可以让你免于无尽的陷阱和大规模的损失。
书：辞去工作前的富爸爸：每个企业家都应该知道的关于建立数百万美元企业的10个现实生活中的教训。
你在这本书中为商人和企业家找到了什么？在商业上取得成功的十种有效且经过验证的方法。这些将不是理论计算，而是对世界市场资源的令人印象深刻的分析。作者谈到了他的成功，承认了他的错误和失败。剩下的就是得出通往成功和繁荣的结论和教训。
“创业者指南”这本书是为那些即将开始创业或已经朝这个方向迈出了第一步的人准备的。Bill Oleta描述了创建创新产品和启动创业的所有阶段，为读者提供了几乎一步步的指导。你能学习创业精神吗？是的，你只需要一本合适的商业书籍。
书：你的初创企业：适应未来，投资于自己，改变你的职业生涯。
商业成功的秘诀之一是思维的灵活性和适应环境的能力。不断寻找新想法、新市场、新产品、新机会--企业家的本能总是引领商人走向成功。你对发展像英特尔、苹果或谷歌这样的初创企业的秘诀感兴趣吗？“创业人生”一定会符合你的兴趣。
理查德·布兰森(Richard Branson)认为，对于商业初学者和企业家来说，最重要的是自力更生、勇气、勇气和奉献精神。如果你准备打破刻板印象，战斗，克服障碍，而不是等待你的一生找到有利的条件，这本书将是你发展自己的巨大动力。
这本书的书名似乎太有看头了，但它的内容不会让你失望的。你是否对“延期生活”和“办公室奴役”条件太熟悉了？那么你肯定需要知道什么是“每周4小时工作制”。了解新的生活方式和成功的新理念！
这位最著名的年轻现代商人之一令人印象深刻的精彩传记令人难以置信地鼓舞人心。Zappos从零到数十亿的道路只用了不到10年的时间。你可以获得关于这是如何发生的第一手信息-关于它所需要的所有计划，关于所有的错误，以及让Zappos成功的环境。
这本书完全致力于理查德·布兰森爵士真正的成功故事。他的写作非常坦率。在商人和企业家眼中，对这个故事特别有价值的是维珍集团创始人这本笔记本中的语录和摘录。
市场营销文献是实验想法的取之不尽的源泉。这是一个面向初学者和高级营销人员的报头。
读完这本书后，神秘的营销术语将不再让你感到害怕。作者(顺便说一句，是诺贝尔奖获得者)将告诉你什么是“慢”和“快”思维，以及它们如何影响我们行为和决策的合理性。
根据传奇人物杰克·特劳特的权威观点，差异化是现代营销成功的基础。面对激烈的竞争，生存本身并不是目的。制定强有力的促销策略，击败竞争对手，打造品牌，在全球营销中取得成功，这是你应该努力的方向。
对于仔细分析目标受众需求的营销者和广告商来说，这本出版物将是一本最有价值的读物。你将能够从一个新的角度来看待消费者的世界，这种观点必然会导致原创营销理念的出现。许多实用资料会给你提供发人深省的食粮。
没有比口碑更好的产品广告了。但是你是怎么得到它的呢？你需要成为一名出色的营销员吗？还是在广告上投入巨资？不用谢。你可以让每个人都谈论你的品牌，并在通俗易懂的实用指南的帮助下，将这些对话转化为有效的推广策略。
商学院没有教授突破性思维，事实上，它与分析思维和固有的灵活性相结合，创造了独特的创造力。对于一个优秀的营销者来说，掌握这些技能是至关重要的。
这本书总是希望手头有定期重读和更新的记忆，它包含了分析机制，相关的投资技巧，以及关于金钱科学的方法论。试图理解这一科学并将其应用于实践，是未来金融福祉的关键。
许多现代交易员和金融分析师将他们的成就归功于艾略特波原理。作者制定的方法可以让读者对证券交易所的变化做出准确的预测，对于任何进行证券交易的人来说都是不可或缺的。
这本关于管理和财务的书将是自我发展的极好工具。它将清除你大脑中“金融负面”的旧刻板印象，并重新配置它的“金钱浪潮”。
财务管理书籍顶部的最后一条是区块链指南，以一种迷人的方式揭示了这项巧妙发明的精髓。
给商人和企业家的好文学作品会给你很多实用的建议和建议，告诉你如何逃离危急关头，教你新知识，如何改变坏习惯，如何形成通向真正成功的思维。

The leaders of the top modern companies were, at one time, startup entrepreneurs who only dreamed of building a profitable business. Each leader had to learn how to manage personnel, how to run a business, how to handle mistakes, and they had to learn from them. The real experiences of these people are of great value for both business newcomers and the experienced entrepreneurs who want to not only keep their company afloat but also bring it to market leadership.
 We’ve created a list of great books on sales, finance, business, and management, each of which is ready to serve as a powerful motivator for entrepreneurs of any level.
   Here is a list of the best business books that are required reading for entrepreneurs. Lessons from the successes of people who have achieved consistently high profits will make you think about the future and how you can start changing it for the better right now.
   This book is written in simple language, but it reveals several interesting, complex, and exciting ideas. The uniqueness of Warren Buffett – the most famous investor in America – is that his billions were acquired not by the development of his company, but by investing in others. What can you learn by reading this book? Buffett’s investment decisions, his tactics, strategies, how he chose the right investments, how he works, and the way a great investor thinks.
   The Richest Man in Babylon is a classic piece of economic literature, but the secrets of success outlined in it apply to any field of activity. Want to learn how to make money? Reading this book will bring you closer to understanding the central and unchanging laws of finance, to comprehending the secret of making profitable investments, and to accumulating, preserving, and increasing your capital.
 Book: First, Break All the Rules: What the World’s Greatest Managers Do Differently
  For practicing managers who study books on sales and personnel management, this book should be a staple on your desk. How do you discover the strengths of your employees? How do you reveal their talents and achieve maximum commitment to their work? Knowledge, abilities, skills, experience, achievements – these concepts should not be confused nor replaced. You cannot demand from people what they are not capable of, but there are ways of creating an ideal working atmosphere on a team, thus ensuring the effective and rapid development of your company.
 Book: The E-Myth Revisited: Why Most Small Businesses Don’t Work and What to Do About It
  This book is a godsend for those who are going to build their business from scratch. What business model will work smoothly, even without its founder? What are the secrets of the development of small enterprises? How do you organize, develop, and improve your business? All of this you will learn in an easy and understandable form.
   This book clearly stands out from other traditional business novels. It teaches you to control your life and deeply analyze emerging problems, it introduces readers to the theory of limitations, and it talks about love and risk. The author’s non-standard thinking has made the book “The Goal” a real tutorial in the search for common sense and a rational approach to life.
   The best books on business are not written every day. All of them have had to pass the test of time to prove their relevance after decades. Pay attention to the selection of books from authors whose authority in business is beyond doubt.
   This book from the management guru himself can be used as a kind of bible for experienced managers and beginners alike. The book, along with its many practical tips, gives global forecasts regarding the development of the world economy and humanity in general. “Management Tasks in the 21st Century” is not dry theory, but an active call to action. It is preparation for the implementation of new tasks and the solution to urgent problems.
   This fantastic book is worth reading to help you understand that not one book, regardless of whether it’s the very best book on business and entrepreneurship, will solve your problems. The key to success is what the author of the bestseller, which has a circulation of more than 10 million copies, sees in successful companies: the understanding of responsibility, in what is right, in constructive thinking, and in unexpected and bold decisions.
   The biography of Steve Jobs was not only a sales hit, but a mega-bestseller. It is based on a huge number of conversations with the creator of Apple himself, along with his relatives, friends and enemies, well-wishers, and competitors. The life story of Steve Jobs is exciting and instructive, surprising, and more importantly, it inspires and makes you think, adapt, and fight.
   The original title of the book by Henry Ford, who changed the world we live in today, is My Life And Work. It is famous all over the world and has more than one hundred editions. Many consider the book to be the biography of Henry Ford himself, but rather, this is the biography of his life’s work. Principles of company development and business creation, ups and downs, risks and mistakes, victories and breakthroughs – everything is here to highlight how complicated, lengthy, and thorny the road to enormous success and global recognition can be.
   Disconnected from print media, young entrepreneurs often look for answers in literature and audio books to the burning question: “How can you start a business from scratch?” – And they do so in vain. Business ideas, tips, doctrines, methodologies, as well as real success stories are often more effective than long, expensive courses that promise to reveal global economic secrets.
   To come up with a business idea and implement it is not the most difficult thing. It is much more difficult to promote a product, get it to the market, and unleash a spiral of growth and development. The rapid testing of new products on real consumers to instantly adjust business development strategies is one of the proposals the author of this book shows you, which can save you from endless pitfalls and large-scale losses.
 Book: Rich Dad’s Before You Quit Your Job: 10 Real-Life Lessons Every Entrepreneur Should Know About Building a Multimillion-Dollar Business
  What do you find in this book for businessmen and entrepreneurs? Ten effective and proven ways to achieve success in business. These will not be theoretical calculations, but an impressive analysis of the resources of the world market. The author talks about his successes and admits his mistakes and failures. All that is left is to draw conclusions and lessons that lead to success and prosperity.
   The “Guide for Entrepreneurs” book is intended for those who are on the verge of starting their own business or who have already taken the first steps in this direction. Bill Oleta describes all the stages of creating an innovative product and launching a startup, offering the reader almost step-by-step instructions. Can you learn entrepreneurship? Yes, you just need the right business literature.
 Book: The Start-Up of You: Adapt to the Future, Invest in Yourself, and Transform Your Career
  One of the secrets to business success is flexibility of thinking and the ability to adapt to circumstances. The constant search for new ideas, markets, products, opportunities – the entrepreneurial instinct always leads businessman to success. Are you interested in the secrets of developing startups like Intel, Apple, or Google? “Life as a Startup” will definitely match your interests.
     For business beginners and entrepreneurs, the most important thing, according to Richard Branson, is self-reliance, courage, boldness, and dedication. If you are ready to break stereotypes, fight, and overcome obstacles instead of waiting for your whole life to find favorable conditions, this book will be a great incentive for you to develop yourself.
   The title of the book seems too promising, but its contents will not disappoint you. Are “deferred life” and “office slavery” conditions that you’re all too familiar with? Then you definitely need to know what “The 4-Hour Work Week” is. Get to know the new lifestyle and new philosophy of success!
   The impressively brilliant biography of one of the most famous young modern businessmen is incredibly inspiring. Zappos’ path from zero to billions took less than 10 years. You can get first-hand information about how this happened – about all the planning it took, about all the mistakes, and circumstance that made Zappos a success.
   This book is entirely devoted to the real success story of Sir Richard Branson. He is remarkably frank in his writing. Of particular value to the story in the eyes of businessmen and entrepreneurs are the quotes and excerpts from this notebook of the founder of the Virgin Group.
   Marketing literature is an inexhaustible source of ideas for experimentation. Here is a paper masthead for beginners and advanced marketers.
   Mysterious marketing terms will stop scaring you after reading this book. The author (a Nobel laureate, by the way) will tell you what “slow” and “fast” thinking is, and how they affect the rationality of our behavior and decision making.
   According to the authoritative opinion of the legendary Jack Trout, differentiation is the basis of successful modern marketing. In the face of fierce competition, survival is not an end in itself. Developing a powerful promotional strategy, beating competitors, creating a brand, and achieving success in global marketing is what you should strive for.
   For marketers and advertisers who meticulously analyze the needs of their target audience, this publication will be a most valuable read. You will be able to look at the consumer’s world from a new angle, and this view will invariably lead to the emergence of original marketing ideas. A lot of practical material will provide you with food for thought.
   There is no better advertising for a product than word of mouth. But how do you get it? Do you need to be a brilliant marketer? Or invest heavily in advertising? Not at all. You can make everyone talk about your brand and turn these conversations into an effective promotion strategy with the help of an accessible and practical guide.
   Breakthrough thinking is not taught in business schools, and in fact, it, in combination with an analytical mindset and inherent flexibility, creates unique creativity. It is vital for a good marketer to master these skills.
     This book, which is always desirable to have on hand to periodically re-read and update in your memory, contain mechanisms for analysis, relevant techniques for investing, and methodologies about the science of money. Attempts to comprehend this science and apply it in practice is the key to financial well-being in the future.
   Many modern traders and financial analysts owe their achievements to Elliott wave principle. The methods formulated by the author allow readers to make accurate predictions about changes on the stock exchanges and are indispensable for anyone who is going to trade in securities.
   This book on management and finance will be an excellent tool for self-development. It will clear your brain of old stereotypes of “financial negative” and reconfigure its “money wave.”
   The last item in the top of the books on financial management is a guide to the blockchain, revealing the very essence of this ingenious invention in a fascinating way.
 Good works of literature for businessmen and entrepreneurs will give you a lot of practical advice and recommendations, show you how to escape critical situations, teach you new knowledge, how to change bad habits, and how to form the thinking that leads to real success.

$\BEGINGROUP$QUICE最终更新：只想感谢MO用户的所有支持。特别感谢你的快速回答，我已经接受了第一个，感谢它带给我的清晰。我已经用${\rmcr}(G)$更新了我的圆环算法。在我的完整测试集上运行良好，即圆环上的${\rmcr}(G)={\rmPCR}(G)$的证据。关于这一点的更多信息，稍后还将从上一个答案开始测试更清晰的界限。我会及时提交的！再次感谢MO用户的帮助！
原帖：如果“危机”这个词用得太重，我道歉，但我现在处于恐慌状态，如果这个词是正确的话：两周后，我应该提交我的博士论文，但我刚刚收到了坏消息，或者我应该说一些让我非常担心的信息。这真的是一种紧急情况：
我的论文是计算机科学，与球面和环面上的图形绘制相关的算法。我依赖的一个重要数学结果是图的边交叉引理(或边交叉不等式)。它给出了图$G$的任意图$G$具有$n$顶点和$e$边${\rmcr}(G)\geq\frac{e^3}{64n^2}$$的最小边交数${\rmcr}(G)$的下界。
问题：我在Pach and Tóth的文章中读到，关于交叉数的数学试卷有可能有不同的定义。有交叉数${\Rmcr}(G)$($G$的最小边交叉数)，也有${\RmPcr}(G)$的对交叉数，即$G$的图中的最小边对交叉数。我仔细检查了我的算法，根据这个定义，我清楚地应用了配对交叉数${\rMPCR}(G)$。
关键问题：你能向我证实边交叉引理在球面和环面上对交叉数${\rMPCR}(G)$也是有效的吗？

$\begingroup$  QUICK FINAL UPDATE: Just wanted to thank you MO users for all your support. Special thanks for the fast answers, I've accepted first one, appreciated the clarity it gave me. I've updated my torus algorithm with  ${\rm cr}(G)$. Works fine on my full test set, i.e. evidence for  ${\rm cr}(G)={\rm pcr}(G)$ on torus. More on this later, will test sharper bound from last answer as well.  I'm going to submit in time! Thanks again MO users for all your help!
 Original post: I apologize if „crisis“ is too strong a word, but I am in a mode of panic, if that's the right word: In two weeks, I should be submitting my Ph.D. Thesis, but I have just received bad news, or I should say information that makes me very concerned. It is really an emergency situation:
 My thesis is in computer science, algorithms related to graph drawings on the sphere and the torus. One of the cornerstone mathematical results I am relying on is the graph edge crossing lemma (or edge crossing inequality). It gives a lower bound for the minimum number of edge crossings  ${\rm cr}(G)$ for any drawing of the graph  $G$ with  $n$ vertices and  $e$ edges $${\rm cr}(G)\geq \frac{e^3}{64n^2}$$for  $e>4n$.
 PROBLEM: I am reading in the article of Pach and Tóth that there is a possibility that mathematics papers on crossing numbers operate with different definitions. There is the crossing number  ${\rm cr}(G)$ (minimum of edge crossings in a drawing of  $G$), but also the  pair crossing number ${\rm pcr}(G)$, the minimum number of edge pairs crossing in a drawing of  $G$. I double-checked my algorithms and, based on this definition, I clearly apply the pair crossing number  ${\rm pcr}(G)$
 CRITICAL QUESTION: Can you confirm to me that the edge crossing lemma remains valid on the sphere and the torus also for the pair crossing number  ${\rm pcr}(G)$?
 Reference: János Pach and Géza Tóth.  Which crossing number is it anyway? J. Combin. Theory Ser. B, 80(2): 225–246, 2000.
      $\endgroup$
  $\begingroup$  I don't really know anything about crossing numbers, but I can appreciate how stressful this must be for you. I hope that you are able to patch things up in time!  $\endgroup$–  Carl-Fredrik Nyberg Brodda
  $\begingroup$  @PerAlexandersson --- as I understand it two edges may intersect multiple times; this multiplicity is counted in cr but not in pcr, hence pcr $\leq$ cr.  $\endgroup$–  Carlo Beenakker

I was put in this position by a letter from Mr. Bertrand Russell when the work was nearly through the press. "...  $\endgroup$–  Toffomat
  $\begingroup$  Maybe you could accept the answer that is here, and then add your own answer after you successfully defend your dissertation. The vast majority of people who read this question are rooting for you.  $\endgroup$–  John Coleman
  $\begingroup$  $\DeclareMathOperator\cr{cr}\DeclareMathOperator\pcr{pcr}$For the pair crossing number  $\pcr(G)$, the short answer is  yes the crossing lemma holds for drawings on the sphere, but it is  not known whether it also holds on the torus.
 The best and most current reference for you could be the survey article from Schaefer, updated in February 2020: “The Graph Crossing Number and its Variants: A Survey” from the Electronic Journal of Combinatorics( https://doi.org/10.37236/2713).
 The relevant pages for you are pages 5 and 6 with the following quote from Schaefer:
 “Since the Hanani–Tutte theorem is not known to be true for the torus, this means that we do not currently have a proof of the crossing lemma for  $\pcr$ or  $\pcr_−$ on the torus.”
 Generally,  $\pcr(G)\leq \cr(G)$. It is still an open problem whether they are equal or not. The first proofs of the crossing lemma did not make the distinction. The first one to raise the ambiguity was Mohar (1995) in a conference talk.
 The  Pach and Tóth (2000) paper that you mention does make the distinction between  $\pcr(G)$ and  $\cr(G)$, and applies Hanani–Tutte in the proof of the crossing lemma, which ensures that it also holds for  $\pcr(G)$.
 The issue is that you can apply Hanani–Tutte for the sphere (and the projective plane), but you cannot apply it for the torus. For surfaces of genus  $\geq4$ it is known to be false, see Fulek and Kynčl (2019). This means the torus is really “in-between”.
  Bojan Mohar (1995): Problem mentioned at the special session on Topological Graph Theory, Mathfest, Burlington, Vermont. (cited from: L.A. Székely (2016):  Turán’s Brick Factory Problem: The Status of the Conjectures of Zarankiewicz and Hill. In: R. Gera et al. (eds.)(2016): Graph Theory—favorite conjectures and open problems. 1.)
  Radoslav Fulek and Jan Kynčl (2019):  Counterexample to an Extension of the Hanani–Tutte Theorem on the Surface of Genus 4. Combinatorica, 39(6):1267–1279
    $\endgroup$
  $\begingroup$  The author's name is Toth by the way, not Thot lol.  $\endgroup$–  Hollis Williams
  $\begingroup$  From OP's point of view this could be viewed as glass half-full rather than glass half-empty. Their dissertation results hold unequivocally on the sphere and  might hold on the torus, though it is an open problem if they do. It is certainly legitimate to study what follows from a given conjecture being true. It could even be spun as a feature rather than a bug of the dissertation.  If the results in fact fail on the torus then you know that the conjecture must be false. Potentially, it could open up a fruitful avenue of attack.  $\endgroup$–  John Coleman

    $\begingroup$  Assuming an unpublished Ramsey-type result by Robertson and Seymour about Kuratowski minors [FK18, Claim 5], which is now "folklore" in the graph-minor community,an asymptotic variant of the crossing lemma,  $\operatorname{cr}(G)\ge \Omega(e^3/n^2)$, is true even for the pair crossing number on a fixed surface, such as a torus.
 With Radoslav Fulek [FK18, Corollary 9] we have shown that [FK18, Claim 5] implies an approximate version of the Hanani–Tutte theorem on orientable surfaces.In particular, [FK18, Claim 5] implies that there is a constant  $g$ such that for every graph  $G$ that can be drawn on the torus with every pair of independent edges crossing an even number of times,  $G$ can be drawn on the orientable surface of genus  $g$ without crossings.This gives an upper bound  $3n + O(g)$ on the number of edges of every such graph  $G$, and this can be used in the probabilistic proof of the crossing lemma, as described on p. 5-6 of Marcus Schaefer's survey [S20], mentioned in Claus Dollinger's answer. See also [SSSV96, Theorem 4.1].
  [FK18]  https://dx.doi.org/10.4230/LIPIcs.SoCG.2018.40,  https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.05085 - R. Fulek and J Kynčl, The  $\mathbb Z_2$-genus of Kuratowski minors
 [SSSV96]  https://doi.org/10.1007/BF02086611 - F. Shahrokhi, L. A. Székely, O. Sýkora and I. Vrt'o, Drawings of graphs on surfaces with few crossings, Algorithmica 16, 118-131 (1996)
 [S20]  https://doi.org/10.37236/2713 - M. Schaefer, The Graph Crossing Number and its Variants: A Survey, The Electronic Journal of Combinatorics, DS21: Feb 14, 2020.
    $\endgroup$

    $\begingroup$  @user161819 I wanted to make a comment but it got too long, so putting it as an answer. But please take it just as a comment for later, once everything is finished:
 If I understand your comment to my answer correctly, you are aiming to change your algorithm for the torus so it works with  ${\rm cr}(G)$. I think the whole MO community is keeping their fingers crossed, wishing you that you can successfully complete everything in time!
 Looking at the far horizon, I wanted to make a suggestion to you. Once you have changed your torus algorithm and completed your thesis, you will have effectively two algorithms in your hands for the torus: The old one based on  ${\rm pcr}(G)$ and the new one based on  ${\rm cr}(G)$. I am saying the obvious here,  keep both of them, they can really be fruitful for future research.
 (1) Obviously, your two algorithms could support research on the big open question whether  ${\rm pcr}(G)\stackrel{\rm ?}{=}{\rm cr}(G)$ or not. Theycould produce experimental evidence, ideas, and insights for afuture proof of equality, or an actual counterexample. (Again, I amsaying the obvious here.)
 (2) To really pressure-test  ${\rm pcr}(G)\stackrel{\rm ?}{=}{\rm cr}(G)$ on the torus, it would be interesting to also try thebest known to date lower bound for  ${\rm cr}(G)$ $$\frac{1}{29}\frac{e^3}{n^2}$$ for graphs with  $e>7n$. This lower bound is fromEyal Ackerman (2019): "On topological graphs with at most fourcrossings per edge", Computational Geometry, 85: 101574, 31,doi:10.1016/j.comgeo.2019.101574 (probably you are aware of it fromthe Wikipedia article that you quoted).
 I think your question and this whole topic are really important. László Székely calls it one of the "foundational problems" and devotes a whole section to it in his article  Turán’s Brick Factory Problem: The Status of the Conjectures of Zarankiewicz and Hill. In: R. Gera et al. (eds.)(2016): Graph Theory—favorite conjectures and open problems. 1.)
    $\endgroup$
  $\begingroup$  Thanks for your comment!! Much appreciated. And thanks again for your help. I'm very interested in this Ackermann bound, will take a look at it  $\endgroup$–  user161819
  $\begingroup$  One good thing, my prototype for updated torus algorithm: tested on on first 2 graphs from my test set, and went ok  $\endgroup$–  user161819
  $\begingroup$  The term "Ackermann bound" generally refers to a bound of a type far, far worse than the one given here:  en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ackermann_function  $\endgroup$–  Terry Tao
  $\begingroup$  @TerryTao Terry you are very right. Huge difference between the Wilhelm Ackermann bound that you mention and the Eyal Ackerman bound I am quoting here! Good to make the distinction.  $\endgroup$–  Claus Dollinger
